claim: Teaching hospitals do not provide better care than non-teaching hospitals.

result.claim: Teaching hospitals do not provide better care than non-teaching hospitals.
result.reasoning: A systematic review (Paper ID: 13906581) directly addresses the claim by comparing patient outcomes in teaching versus non-teaching healthcare structures. The review concludes that "the available data... do not suggest that a healthcare facility's teaching status on its own markedly improves or worsens patient outcomes." Specifically, for mortality, the summary relative risk for teaching versus non-teaching healthcare structures was 0.96 (95% confidence interval [CI], 0.93–1.00), indicating no significant difference. The review also states that for other diverse outcomes, "typically teaching healthcare structures did not do better than nonteaching ones." While the study acknowledges limitations due to its nonrandomized design, its comprehensive nature as a systematic review provides strong evidence.

evidence.text: The study reviewed all studies that compared teaching versus nonteaching healthcare structures.
evidence.source: Patient Outcomes with Teaching Versus Nonteaching Healthcare: A Systematic Review

evidence.text: The study was conducted at a major public teaching hospital in Switzerland.
evidence.source: Dexamethasone and risk of nausea and vomiting and postoperative bleeding after tonsillectomy in children: a randomized trial.

evidence.text: The summary relative risk for teaching versus nonteaching healthcare structures for mortality was 0.96 (95% confidence interval [CI], 0.93–1.00).
evidence.source: Patient Outcomes with Teaching Versus Nonteaching Healthcare: A Systematic Review

evidence.text: The study was conducted in medical wards in 14 hospitals in the United Kingdom.
evidence.source: Cost effectiveness of ward based non-invasive ventilation for acute exacerbations of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease: economic analysis of randomised controlled trial.

evidence.text: The study was conducted in 23 academic and community hospitals throughout Canada.
evidence.source: Clinical outcomes following institution of the Canadian universal leukoreduction program for red blood cell transfusions.

evidence.text: A typical UK hospital providing a non-invasive ventilation service will avoid six deaths per year.
evidence.source: Cost effectiveness of ward based non-invasive ventilation for acute exacerbations of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease: economic analysis of randomised controlled trial.

evidence.text: Unadjusted in-hospital mortality rates were 7.03% during the control period.
evidence.source: Clinical outcomes following institution of the Canadian universal leukoreduction program for red blood cell transfusions.

evidence.text: The study was conducted in a teaching and non-teaching hospital.
evidence.source: Ethnographic study of incidence and severity of intravenous drug errors.

evidence.text: A typical UK hospital providing a non-invasive ventilation service will avoid three to nine admissions to intensive care units per year.
evidence.source: Cost effectiveness of ward based non-invasive ventilation for acute exacerbations of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease: economic analysis of randomised controlled trial.

evidence.text: The P-value for the difference in in-hospital mortality rates was 0.04.
evidence.source: Clinical outcomes following institution of the Canadian universal leukoreduction program for red blood cell transfusions.
